features.name: HTTP/HTTPS single-stream downloads
features.notes: Modern engine downloads HTTP/HTTPS resources and publishes progress.

features.name: Resume partial downloads
features.notes: Uses Range and If-Range with persisted validators.

features.name: Multi-connection segmented acceleration
features.notes: Concurrent bounded ranges, resumable segment files, deterministic merge and safe single-stream fallback are implemented.

features.name: Fallback when Range is ignored
features.notes: Restarts safely as a single stream.

features.name: Retry transient failures
features.notes: Bounded exponential retry with jitter.

features.name: Validator and content-range integrity checks
features.notes: ETag, Last-Modified and Content-Range are validated.

features.name: Disk space preflight
features.notes: Checks expected remaining size with a safety margin.

features.name: Crash-safe finalization
features.notes: Uses durable finalization markers.

features.name: Unknown-length response support
features.notes: Unknown-length HTTP responses fall back from segmented probing to the single-stream path and publish the observed final length.

features.name: HTTP proxy and proxy authentication
features.notes: System, direct, and manual proxy modes include bounded ports, bypass rules, and optional proxy authentication. The configured handler is created after settings initialization.

features.name: FTP/FTPS downloads
features.notes: Legacy FTP/FTPS transport is explicitly replaced by HTTPS downloads or a dedicated file-transfer client; the modern engine rejects those schemes with an actionable message.

features.name: Add a single download
features.notes: Avalonia form creates normal downloads.

features.name: Batch URL input
features.notes: Multiline parsing is implemented.

features.name: Headers, cookies, referer and basic authentication
features.notes: Request metadata is applied without shelling out.

features.name: Duplicate destination policy
features.notes: Auto-rename, overwrite and skip are represented.

features.name: Queue execution and ordering
features.notes: Queue membership, ordering and runtime controls are active.

features.name: Per-download priority
features.notes: Low, normal and high priorities persist with each download and order queued starts before stable queue position.

features.name: Day/time scheduling including overnight windows
features.notes: Multiple timezone-aware day/time windows run independently and preserve overnight behavior.

features.name: Shutdown, sleep, exit and command completion actions
features.notes: Queue completion can exit XDM, run a direct executable, or request supported shutdown, sleep, hibernate and logout actions after a cancellable countdown.

features.name: Multiple independent queue schedules
features.notes: Up to 64 independently enabled schedule definitions can target separate queues.

features.name: Persisted missed-run policy
features.notes: Skip and run-immediately policies use an atomic persisted evaluation checkpoint to avoid duplicate windows.

features.name: Completion-action platform capability checks
features.notes: The UI reports availability and the service rejects unavailable platform actions before execution.

features.name: Authenticated loopback capture service
features.notes: Authenticated loopback capture waits for an explicit queue decision and redacts URL query/fragment data from status.

features.name: Native messaging host installation and repair
features.notes: Protocol 2.0 host supports install/repair/uninstall, health and compatibility inspection for Firefox and Chromium-family browsers.

features.name: Cancel browser download after XDM acceptance
features.notes: Automatic browser downloads are cancelled and erased only after the native host receives XDM queue acceptance.

features.name: Download all links workflow
features.notes: Context menus provide Download with XDM, media capture and bounded Download all links batches.

features.name: Per-site, MIME and size interception rules
features.notes: Shared deterministic rules cover enable/temporary disable, minimum size, MIME, extension, included/excluded sites and incognito policy.

features.name: Direct media URL probing
features.notes: Direct HTTP media is catalogued and written atomically through the unified media pipeline.

features.name: HLS discovery and download
features.notes: Master and media playlists, renditions, byte ranges, AES-128, live refresh, checkpoints, retry and deterministic assembly are implemented.

features.name: DASH discovery and download
features.notes: Static and dynamic MPDs, SegmentTemplate timelines, SegmentList, representation selection, checkpoints and manifest refresh are implemented.

features.name: yt-dlp extractor provider
features.notes: yt-dlp is discovered safely, queried using structured JSON, and receives sensitive metadata through a private temporary configuration file.

features.name: FFmpeg discovery and diagnostics
features.notes: FFmpeg discovery, health diagnostics and no-shell stream-copy muxing are implemented.

features.name: Audio extraction and MP3 conversion
features.notes: Fixed capability presets extract audio through libmp3lame with validated streams, structured progress, cancellation and atomic publication.

features.name: MP4 remux/transcode
features.notes: MP4 remux and H.264/AAC transcode presets validate stream compatibility and publish output atomically.

features.name: Atomic settings persistence
features.notes: Atomic save, backup and corrupt-file quarantine are implemented.

features.name: Import XDM 8 settings and queues
features.notes: Modern JSON plus legacy JSON, Java properties, XML, and conventional legacy directories are imported through bounded normalization with recorded fixtures.

features.name: Download history persistence
features.notes: History has atomic persistence and backup recovery.

features.name: Move, rename, delete and re-download actions
features.notes: History-only removal is distinct from destructive file deletion; completed files can be moved/renamed, opened/revealed, and safely queued again.

features.name: Bounded history retention and pruning
features.notes: Optional age/count retention prunes terminal history only; downloaded files are never deleted automatically.

features.name: Refresh expired URLs and source-page actions
features.notes: Failed/paused entries can replace an expired HTTP(S) URL without discarding partial data; source pages and destinations are durable and actionable.

features.name: Download-list import and export
features.notes: Versioned JSON and plain URL lists are bounded to 8 MiB/50,000 entries; credentials, cookies, and authorization data are never exported.

features.name: Large-history responsive loading and updates
features.notes: Startup restores history in one state publication and indexed lookup avoids full sort/search work for every individual update.

features.name: Category and compound-extension rules
features.notes: Configurable categories and compound extensions are supported.

features.name: Clipboard URL monitoring
features.notes: Monitoring and optional auto-add are available.

features.name: Single-instance activation
features.notes: Second launches activate the existing window.

features.name: Tray/background lifecycle
features.notes: Window close keeps downloads running; tray can restore or exit.

features.name: Completion and failure notifications
features.notes: Desktop notifications are abstracted and tested.

features.name: Redacted diagnostic bundle
features.notes: Bounded structured diagnostics with redaction.

features.name: Safe mode and crash recovery
features.notes: Unclean shutdown detection and safe mode exist.

features.name: Automatic antivirus scanning
features.notes: Completed scheduled files can be scanned by an absolute configured executable with bounded arguments, timeout and no shell expansion.

features.name: Legacy translation migration
features.notes: Retained language packs are parsed through bounded stable keys with English fallback and a modern embedded resource catalog.

features.name: Keyboard and screen-reader navigation
features.notes: The modern shell provides tab navigation, accessible names, a polite live status region, direct focus shortcuts, section shortcuts, and cancellation shortcuts.

features.name: Self-contained Windows and Linux packages
features.notes: Linux and Windows publish/qualification scripts exist.

features.name: In-app update channel
features.notes: Self-modifying in-process updates are replaced by externally installed, checksum-qualified release packages from the HTTPS release channel.

features.name: Versioned and constrained native messaging protocol
features.notes: Strict schemas, fixed message and batch limits, per-connection session authentication and an explicit command allow-list prevent arbitrary command execution.

features.name: Safe browser request metadata forwarding
features.notes: Cookies, referer, user agent, filename, MIME, size, bounded safe headers and bounded POST metadata are validated end to end.

features.name: Firefox and Chromium-family extension parity
features.notes: Firefox and Chromium-compatible builds cover Chrome/Chromium, Edge, Brave, Vivaldi and Opera-compatible native-host locations.

features.name: Audio/video representation selection
features.notes: The Avalonia workflow exposes deterministic video, audio and subtitle selections from native and provider catalogs.

features.name: Subtitle discovery and sidecar download
features.notes: HLS and yt-dlp subtitle tracks are selectable and saved as language-labelled sidecar files.

features.name: Live HLS and DASH capture
features.notes: Live manifests refresh until the bounded user-selected capture duration is reached.

features.name: Fragment retries and resumable checkpoints
features.notes: Fragment completion is checkpointed atomically and retries are bounded with cancellation-aware backoff.

features.name: HLS AES-128 decryption
features.notes: Identity-key AES-128 HLS is supported with explicit or media-sequence-derived IVs; unsupported DRM schemes are rejected.

features.name: Capability presets and codec/container validation
features.notes: User choices resolve only to fixed preset IDs; FFprobe validates streams before FFmpeg starts.

features.name: Conversion queue, progress and cancellation
features.notes: Jobs run sequentially, parse structured FFmpeg progress and terminate the process tree on cancellation.

features.name: Post-download conversion workflow
features.notes: Completed downloads can be sent to the queue and media downloads can optionally enqueue a conversion preset automatically.

features.name: Safe FFmpeg invocation and atomic output
features.notes: ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList is used with shell execution disabled; private temporary output is atomically moved only after success.

features.name: Timeout, retry, and segmented-transfer defaults
features.notes: Connection/request timeouts, retry attempts/delay, default/max connections, and segmented-transfer minimum size are bounded and persisted.

features.name: Versioned settings import and export
features.notes: Atomic versioned exports redact secrets by default; modern and legacy imports reuse schema normalization.

features.name: Host credential manager
features.notes: Exact-host and opt-in subdomain credentials fill only otherwise unspecified request authentication; exports redact passwords by default.

features.name: Download dialog and workflow defaults
features.notes: Default duplicate behavior, connection count, directory creation, category selection, clipboard behavior, and request-metadata preference are persisted in the modern schema.

features.name: Runtime language switching and persisted preference
features.notes: Language selection and system-language preference are stored in schema 5 and refresh the active Avalonia surface after settings are saved.

features.name: Right-to-left layout support
features.notes: Culture metadata identifies RTL languages and updates the inherited window flow direction at runtime.

features.name: Locale-aware values and status presentation
features.notes: Byte counts, rates, durations, aggregate speed, and timeline values use the selected culture.

features.name: High contrast, scaling, and live status preferences
features.notes: Persisted 75–175% scaling, high-contrast styles, and optional polite operation announcements update the active window.

features.name: Executable final parity certification
features.notes: Critical and high contracts must be fully qualified and all completed evidence must resolve inside the repository.

features.name: Representative legacy migration qualification matrix
features.notes: Recorded XML, JSON, history, scheduler and secret-redaction fixtures qualify representative upgrades.

features.name: Linux and Windows final-gate CI
features.notes: Both supported desktop operating-system families build, test, bootstrap and smoke their native package in CI.

features.name: Legacy application source removal
features.notes: Known WPF, GTK, WinForms, legacy CoreFx, compatibility and legacy test application paths are absent and prohibited from the active solution.
